fresh_facts.claim: The United States launched Operation Epic Fury on March 7, 2026, striking over 1,000 Iranian targets in the first 24 hours using more than 100 aircraft, Tomahawk missiles, and cyber operations.
As of March 9, 2026, Operation Epic Fury continued for approximately 57 hours, with two carrier strike groups (USS Gerald R. Ford and USS Abraham Lincoln) positioned in the region.
Vermont Air National Guard's 158th Fighter Wing with F-35s was deployed from New England as part of ongoing operations against Iran.
Iran launched ballistic missiles that penetrated air defenses and struck a US tactical operations center and positions near US Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain.
Three F-15E aircraft were lost in non-hostile incidents and four American deaths occurred during Operation Epic Fury, with one death resulting from an Iranian missile strike.
Mojtaba Khamenei was confirmed as Iran's new Supreme Leader following the death of his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed in bombing approximately two weeks prior.
In his first public statement, Mojtaba Khamenei vowed to continue Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and threatened to attack US bases while pledging to 'avenge the blood of its martyrs.'

Maryam Rajavi, President-elect of the National Council of Resistance of Iran (NCRI), characterized Mojtaba Khamenei's appointment as placing the leader's son on the throne to save a delegitimized regime.

Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian outlined three non-negotiable conditions for ending the war: formal recognition of Iran's sovereign rights, unprecedented financial reparations, and binding international security guarantees.
Approximately two weeks of bombing killed around 2,000 people in Iran and damaged much of Iran's military and security apparatus as of March 12, 2026.
US and Israeli combined forces struck the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) Imam Sadegh Unit in Bushehr Province and military bases as part of the broader campaign.
Iran attacked and hit two foreign oil tankers as of March 12, 2026, as part of its response to military strikes.
US intelligence assessments indicate Iran's government remains structurally intact and is not expected to collapse in the near future despite mounting geopolitical pressure.
Mohammad Mohaddesin, head of foreign policy at the NCRI, stated that bombings cannot overthrow the regime and that only a popular uprising backed by internal resistance could do so.
Thousands of Iranians were reportedly killed during nationwide protests in January 2026 preceding Mojtaba Khamenei's selection as Supreme Leader.
The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has tightened its grip on power and threatened to crush any unrest in response to ongoing military conflict.
Air defenses integrated with partners in Qatar, UAE, Kuwait, Jordan, and Saudi Arabia have intercepted hundreds of Iranian ballistic missiles and drones.
Iranian President Pezeshkian has been in active diplomatic consultations with Russian President Vladimir Putin regarding the Iran-US conflict.
President Trump has remained defiant regarding rising oil prices resulting from Iran's Strait of Hormuz blockade.
The conflict between the US and Iran has created global disruptions to energy markets and transport, spreading conflict across the Middle East.

regime_fall.current_status: Operation Epic Fury has entered day 6-7 with sustained US-Israeli strikes exceeding 1,000 targets in initial 24 hours, followed by two carrier strike groups and regional air defense integration intercepting hundreds of Iranian missiles. Mojtaba Khamenei's succession as Supreme Leader demonstrates regime institutional continuity despite ~2,000 deaths, while US intelligence assesses structural stability remains intact despite his defiant vows to continue Strait blockade and attacks on US bases.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Mojtaba Khamenei confirmed as Iran's new Supreme Leader on March 12, 2026, following his father Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's death in bombing, vowing to continue resistance and Strait of Hormuz blockade while Iranian President Pezeshkian outlined three non-negotiable conditions for ceasefire.
